constraints.trainingRun: CPU-only timing alignment and signed lag search

splitDefinition.method: Stable Fisher-Yates shuffle over scenario indices using Mulberry32 seed 0xc0def00d.

evaluationDesign.causalHistory: referencePoll rows with elapsedMicroseconds <= anchor elapsedMicroseconds
evaluationDesign.label: interpolated referencePoll at anchor + requested horizon; internal prediction horizon may differ
evaluationDesign.signedLagLead: projection of prediction-target error onto causal recent motion direction; negative means lag

terms: tail regression penalties versus Step 3, Step 4, and Step 5 references
timingObjective.guardrail: reject p95 > best reference +0.25px, p99 > best reference +0.5px, resume p95 > +0.5px, resume p99 > +1px, or >10px > +0.0005

interpretation: `step4_gain_1p15` was validation-selected, but test shows tail regression in normal. Timing offset alone is not production-safe; it can move signed bias while increasing tail risk.
nextStepRecommendation: Step 7 should prioritize additional causal observability: explicit prediction-target timestamp, present/compositor timestamp, hold/resume transition markers, and missing-scheduler/warm-up flags. Keep Step 3/5 as safety references until timing telemetry can disambiguate model error from display-time offset.
